name: Kansas Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Kansas — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description: 900 SW Jackson St, Suite 556, Topeka, KS 66612

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (expires October 1st of even-numbered years)

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Kansas require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Kansas does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Kansas.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Kansas. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
